release_review.summary: Managed Browser Editor installation now accepts current code-server packages by raising the workspace engine archive and extraction guard from 256 MiB to 512 MiB.
checked_surfaces: Workspace engine artifact manifest validation keeps sha256, compressed size, compression, platform, and binary path checks unchanged
Workspace engine archive extraction keeps path traversal, absolute path, unsafe path, and hard link/symlink rejection unchanged
Browser Editor package installation remains driven by the Desktop-prepared archive and manifest upload flow
Current code-server release package size fits inside the updated extraction guard while preserving the zip-bomb safety boundary
Desktop package cache, upload bootstrap, SSH runtime bootstrap, Local UI health, sys.ping, and startup report consumers do not require Runtime Service protocol changes
Runtime Service compatibility contract fields and protocol version remain unchanged
release_review.same_window_rationale: The release only changes the local workspace engine archive size guard used after Desktop has already prepared and uploaded a verified Browser Editor package. It does not add, remove, or rename Runtime Service JSON fields, does not change the protocol version, and keeps Desktop attach/start, Local UI health, sys.ping, provider link, runtime-control, SSH runtime bootstrap, and startup report consumers on the existing compatibility window.
